questions.prompt: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.fact: Water's formula H₂O means two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.

questions.prompt: What force keeps planets in orbit around the Sun?
questions.fact: Gravity is described by Newton's law of universal gravitation — every mass attracts every other mass.

questions.prompt: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.fact: Babies are born with around 270 bones; many fuse together as we grow, leaving 206 by adulthood.

questions.prompt: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.fact: Mitochondria produce ATP (adenosine triphosphate), the cell's main energy currency, through cellular respiration.

questions.prompt: In what year did World War II end?
questions.fact: WWII ended in 1945 — V-E Day (May 8) marked victory in Europe, and V-J Day (September 2) marked Japan's surrender.

questions.prompt: Which ancient wonder of the world still stands today?
questions.fact: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the oldest and only surviving wonder of the ancient world, built around 2560 BC.

questions.prompt: Who was the first President of the United States?
questions.fact: George Washington was inaugurated on April 30, 1789, in New York City, the nation's first capital.

questions.prompt: The Berlin Wall fell in which year?
questions.fact: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, after East Germany opened its borders — a pivotal moment in ending the Cold War.

questions.prompt: What is the largest continent by area?
questions.fact: Asia covers about 44.6 million km² — roughly 30% of Earth's total land area and home to 60% of the world's population.

questions.prompt: Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
questions.fact: Canada's coastline stretches approximately 202,080 km, largely thanks to its thousands of islands and inlets.

questions.prompt: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.fact: Canberra was purpose-built as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne. It became the capital in 1913.

questions.prompt: The Amazon River flows primarily through which country?
questions.fact: About 60% of the Amazon River and rainforest lies within Brazil. It is the world's largest river by water volume.

questions.prompt: Which film franchise features the character 'Jack Sparrow'?
questions.fact: Captain Jack Sparrow, played by Johnny Depp, debuted in Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl (2003).

questions.prompt: In 'The Lion King', what is the name of Simba's father?
questions.fact: Mufasa, voiced by James Earl Jones in the 1994 original, is considered one of Disney's most iconic characters.

questions.prompt: Which artist has the most Grammy wins of all time?
questions.fact: Beyoncé holds the record for most Grammy wins with 32, surpassing classical conductor Georg Solti's 31.

questions.prompt: How many players are on a standard soccer (football) team on the field?
questions.fact: Each team fields 11 players — one goalkeeper and 10 outfield players. The Laws of the Game set this at a minimum of 7.

questions.prompt: In which sport would you perform a 'slam dunk'?
questions.fact: The slam dunk was popularized in the NBA and is considered one of the most exciting plays in basketball.

questions.prompt: Which country won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
questions.fact: Uruguay hosted and won the inaugural FIFA World Cup, defeating Argentina 4–2 in the final in Montevideo.

questions.prompt: How many letters are in the English alphabet?
questions.fact: The English alphabet has 26 letters. The word 'alphabet' itself comes from the first two Greek letters: alpha and beta.

questions.prompt: What is the most widely spoken language in the world by total speakers?
questions.fact: English is the most widely spoken language overall when counting both native and non-native speakers — roughly 1.5 billion people.
